channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Remembering Magufuli: Leadership Lessons and Legacy in Tanzania
item.description: Former Prime Minister Kassim Majaliwa has honoured the late President John Pombe Magufuli during a memorial service in Chato, marking five years since his passing. Majaliwa described Magufuli as a ded...

item.title: President Marcos Orders Temporary Suspension of Fare Increase
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. has directed the Department of Transportation (DOTr) to temporarily suspend the planned fare increase for public transportation. In a video message released o...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran conflict: Iranian attacks, economic impact on Australia, and regional security concerns
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Iran has escalated, with significant casualties among Iranian officials and increased regional tensions. An Iranian missile struck near the Al Minhad Air Base i...

item.title: U.S. government justifies blacklisting AI firm Anthropic over national security concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has filed a court response defending the Pentagon’s decision to blacklist Anthropic, the developer of the Claude AI assistant, citing national security concerns. Defense Secre...

item.title: Morocco Awarded AFCON 2025 Title After Senegal Forfeits Final
item.description: Morocco has been officially declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) after a controversial final against Senegal. The CAF Appeals Board reversed Senegal's initial victory, ruling...

item.title: Trust Issues Among Experts Delay Scientific Analysis of Kushan Coins Found at Mohenjo Daro
item.description: A hoard of ancient coins, weighing 5.55 kilograms and believed to date back to the Kushan Ruler Vasudeva-I era (around 200-225 AD), remains stuck at the Mohenjo Daro site laboratory despite a decision...

item.title: Medical Negligence in Nigeria: Lessons from the Adichie Case and the Need for Systemic Reform
item.description: This opinion piece discusses the suspension of three doctors by the Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN) after the tragic death of Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's young son, which many see as medic...

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies Cantonment Board Employees Cannot Appeal to FST
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has clarified that employees of cantonment boards are not considered civil servants, and therefore cannot approach the Federal Service Tribunal (FST) over serv...

item.title: Federal government takes case to Supreme Court over Emergencies Act use during Freedom Convoy
item.description: The federal government has escalated its legal fight over the invocation of the Emergencies Act during the 2022 Freedom Convoy protests by appealing to the Supreme Court of Canada. The protests, which...

item.title: Electoral Reform Advocates Register Numerous Candidates Ahead of Terrebonne Byelection
item.description: A group known as the Longest Ballot Committee is preparing to field numerous protest candidates in the upcoming Terrebonne federal byelection, one of three scheduled in April. The organisation, which ...

item.title: Tribute to Len Deighton, Influential British Spy Novelist, Who Dies Aged 97
item.description: Len Deighton, a pioneering British author who reshaped the spy novel, has passed away at the age of 97. Known for his debut thriller 'The Ipcress File', which was adapted into a 1965 film starring Mic...

item.title: House Justice Panel Approves Impeachment Complaints Against Sara Duterte; Formal Hearings Set for March 25
item.description: The House of Representatives' Justice Committee has declared that the impeachment complaints filed against Sara Duterte have sufficient grounds to proceed. This decision paves the way for the 'hearing...

item.title: PSG eliminate Chelsea from UEFA Champions League with 3-0 second-leg win for 8-2 aggregate triumph
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain, the French champions and defending UEFA Champions League holders, completely dominated Chelsea in the second leg of their Round of 16 tie at Stamford Bridge. PSG won the match 3-0...

item.title: President of Nigeria Undertakes Historic UK State Visit with Focus on Economic and Cultural Ties
item.description: King Charles III will host President Bola Ahmed Tinubu of Nigeria and his wife Oluremi Tinubu in the United Kingdom, marking the first Nigerian state visit in 37 years. The two-day visit includes cere...

item.title: Bethany Peaty reconciles with her mother amid family tensions surrounding Adam Peaty's wedding
item.description: Bethany Peaty, sister of Olympic swimmer Adam Peaty, has spoken publicly for the first time about the ongoing family dispute involving the Ramsay family and her mother, Caroline. Bethany, who attended...

item.title: Vice-President Calls Tanzanians to Uphold Magufuli’s Legacy of Courage and Self-Reliance
item.description: In Chato, Vice-President Ambassador Dr Emmanuel Nchimbi urged all Tanzanians to embrace selflessness, courage and self-reliance in honour of the late President Dr John Pombe Magufuli. Speaking at a fi...

item.title: Iran vows decisive retaliation after killing of security chief Ali Larijani as missile strikes hit Israel and Strait of Hormuz remains closed
item.description: The ongoing war between the US, Israel, and Iran, now in its third week following strikes that began on 28 February 2026, has seen significant escalation. Iran's army chief, Amir Hatami, promised a 'd...

item.title: Kerala schools face delay in receiving funds for midday meal programme
item.description: Schools across Kerala are experiencing delays in receiving funds for the midday meal scheme, with payments pending since December 2025. Teachers in several schools, including a school in Kasaragod wit...

item.title: Oilers Win 5-3 Over Sharks Despite Draisaitl’s Absence
item.description: The Edmonton Oilers secured a crucial 5-3 victory over the San Jose Sharks on March 17, 2026, despite missing star forward Leon Draisaitl, who is sidelined for the rest of the regular season with a lo...

item.title: Caufield Nets Overtime Goal as Canadiens Defeat Bruins 3-2
item.description: Cole Caufield scored an overtime goal with just 22 seconds remaining, securing a 3-2 victory for the Montreal Canadiens over the Boston Bruins in a closely contested Atlantic Division matchup. Caufiel...
